metadata.description: A foundational network segment identifier — a VLAN (IEEE 802.1Q) or an overlay VNI (VXLAN/Geneve) — owned and advertised by a network/fabric provider and/or defined in a platform data layer, and SELECTED by reference from the resources that ride it (a Network.VirtualNetwork, a Hardware.NetworkInterface). A root resource (docs/foundational-resources.md): dependents reference an existing VLAN, they do not invent one. The concrete encapsulation is provider data; a provider may offer a variant (e.g. Network.Port) that the org ratifies.

context.purpose: Names a network segment — an 802.1Q VLAN or an overlay VNI — once, as the shared object everything that rides it references.
context.plain_description: The segment itself: its encapsulation (802.1Q tag, VXLAN/Geneve VNI, or flat/untagged) and its id. It exists so a segment id appears in exactly one record — switch ports, host sub-interfaces, gateway segments, and virtual networks all reference the VLAN record rather than each retyping the tag. It is a root resource: dependents select an existing VLAN, they do not invent one inline.
use_when: You need one authoritative record per segment that ports, virtual networks, and gateway segments all reference.
You need overlay segments (VXLAN/Geneve VNIs) modeled with the same shape as 802.1Q VLANs.
not_for: The workload attachment point — Network.VirtualNetwork rides a VLAN; guests attach to the VirtualNetwork, not to the VLAN.
A port's tagging configuration — that is vlan_memberships on Hardware.NetworkInterface, referencing this record.
works_with: Hardware.NetworkInterface — ports and sub-interfaces declare membership by reference.
Network.VirtualNetwork — virtual networks ride a referenced segment.
Network.Switch — the fabric carrying the segment.
Network.Gateway — edge segments each ride a referenced VLAN.

encapsulation.description: Segmentation type. vlan = 802.1Q; vxlan/geneve = overlay VNI; flat = untagged.

segment_id.description: The VLAN id (1-4094 for 802.1Q) or VNI (0-16777215 for VXLAN/Geneve). Omitted for flat.

name.description: Human name for the segment, e.g. dmz.

segment_ready.description: Whether the segment is provisioned on the fabric and available to attach.

relationships.name: fabric
relationships.description: The switch(es)/fabric that carry this segment (where the provider manages it).

relationships.name: scope
relationships.description: Optional cluster/fabric scope the segment is defined within.
